steps.title: Use the vertical-shift shortcut
steps.content: Data set B subtracts $9$ from every $y$-value in data set A, so the entire line of best fit shifts down $9$ units. The slope is unchanged, and the $y$-intercept is $9$ less.

steps.title: Adjust the intercept
steps.content: The drawn line for data set A has $y$-intercept about $54.57$. Shift it down by $9$: $54.57-9=45.57$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The $y$-intercept for data set B is closest to $45.57$, so choice A is correct.
